content: I'm thinking about getting a dog, but I'm not sure what breed would work best with my lifestyle. I work from home most days, but I'm gone for 6-8 hours when I go into the office in downtown Portland.
That's a great question! Since you have a flexible schedule, you have several good options. What's your living situation like? Do you have a yard, or are you in an apartment?

I have a small yard in my house here in the Laurelhurst neighborhood. My parents live with me too - they're both in their 80s and I help take care of them while working full-time at my blockchain company.

That's wonderful that you're able to support your parents. For a multi-generational household, you might want to consider a gentle, low-energy breed. Have you thought about size preferences or grooming requirements?

I'd prefer something medium-sized that doesn't shed too much. I already have enough to clean up with my parents around! Plus I'm gone running marathons most weekends, so the dog would need to be okay alone sometimes.

A standard poodle could be perfect! They're intelligent, don't shed much, and can adapt to being alone for reasonable periods. Plus they have the stamina for your marathon training. Have you ever owned a dog before?
No, this would be my first dog. I've been putting it off for years because of my parents and work, but I finally have some breathing room at my DeFi protocol job. The flexible hours help a lot.

That's exciting! With your first dog, you might want to consider adopting an adult dog rather than a puppy. They're often already house-trained and have established temperaments. Would you be open to adoption?
Definitely open to adoption. I've been checking the Oregon Humane Society website. My parents actually had a poodle mix when I was growing up, so I have some familiarity with the breed.
That's perfect! Poodles are great family dogs and tend to be good with older adults since they're gentle and intuitive. When are you hoping to bring a dog home?

I'm aiming for next month after I finish this big smart contract audit at work. I need to puppy-proof the house and set up my home automation system to include the dog's needs - automated feeder, cameras, that sort of thing.

That's very thoughtful! Smart home integration for pets is becoming really popular. You might want to look into GPS trackers too, especially if you're running marathons and want to bring your dog along for training runs.
Great idea! I've been DJing at local events on weekends too, so I need a dog that can handle some noise. Thanks for all the suggestions - I think I'm leaning toward a standard poodle from a rescue.
